prompt:
We finally got sign-off to replace the pile of copy-pasted fleet scripts with one real PowerShell module, and I want it done properly this time: a manifest, a public surface we can document, and everything else private. Working name is OpsKit. The acceptance harness (test_opskit.ps1, read-only, plus everything under fixtures/) is already agreed with the platform team; `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_opskit.ps1` green is the definition of done.

Shape: a module directory `OpsKit/` in the repo root, imported as `Import-Module ./OpsKit/OpsKit.psd1`. The .psd1 must pass Test-ModuleManifest and the module name is OpsKit. Exactly four commands may be visible after import -- Import-OpsInventory, Compare-OpsState, New-OpsPlan, Invoke-OpsPlan -- and nothing else (no exported aliases or variables; helper functions stay module-private). How you split the implementation into files inside OpsKit/ is your call; we care that the export discipline holds and the internals stay navigable, not about any particular file count.

The pipeline these four commands implement is our inventory -> drift -> plan -> apply loop. Host state lives in per-host JSON files (a collector drops them off): each file is one object { host, role, settings } where settings is a flat map of string values. Desired state is one JSON file: { "roles": { "<role>": { "settings": { ... } } } }.

Import-OpsInventory -Path <dir>: read every *.json directly in <dir> (no recursion), processing files in ordinal filename order. A file that is not valid JSON or is missing any of host/role/settings => throw "OpsKit: invalid inventory file '<filename>'" (bare filename, not the path). Two files claiming the same host => throw "OpsKit: duplicate host '<host>'". Output: one object per host with properties Host, Role, Settings, sorted by Host ordinal. Settings must come back as a property bag ([pscustomobject]) -- downstream code walks $x.Settings.PSObject.Properties.

Compare-OpsState -Desired <file> with the inventory objects supplied either by pipeline or via -Inventory (both must behave identically). A host whose role has no entry under roles in the desired file => throw "OpsKit: no desired state for role '<role>'". For each host, compare its settings against its role's desired settings, case-sensitively. Emit one drift object per difference with properties Host, Setting, Kind, Expected, Actual: Kind 'value' when both sides have the setting but values differ (Expected = desired, Actual = host), 'missing' when only desired has it (Actual = $null), 'extra' when only the host has it (Expected = $null). Order: Host ordinal, then Setting ordinal. No drift => nothing emitted. Every property must exist on every object even when $null -- StrictMode consumers.

New-OpsPlan turns drift into an ordered plan; input by pipeline or -Drift (identical behavior). missing and value drift become Action 'set' with Value = Expected; extra becomes Action 'clear' with Value = $null. The plan lists every set step first, then every clear step; inside each group order by Host ordinal then Setting ordinal. Steps carry Order (1-based across the whole plan), Host, Action, Setting, Value. Empty drift => empty plan.

Invoke-OpsPlan -Plan <steps> -Root <dir> applies a plan to the state files in <dir> (the same per-host JSON shape) and must declare SupportsShouldProcess: under -WhatIf not a single byte on disk changes. Before touching anything, verify every host in the plan has a <Root>/<host>.json; if not, throw "OpsKit: no state file for host '<host>'" and leave every file alone. Then apply steps in Order: set writes the value into the host's settings, clear removes the key, and the file is rewritten as JSON (your formatting -- it just has to round-trip through Import-OpsInventory). Emit one result object per applied step: Order, Host, Action, Setting, Status = 'applied'. After a full apply the drift against the same desired file must be zero -- the harness checks convergence end to end.

All five error messages above are thrown as terminating errors, exactly as written. Set-StrictMode -Version Latest must hold throughout the module. Every pinned ordering is ordinal (StringComparer.Ordinal) -- no linguistic sorts anywhere.
notes: 6P9 item 1: OpsKit capstone module -- greenfield inventory/drift/plan/apply pipeline as a proper module; the agent designs the whole OpsKit/ layout (manifest, psm1, public/private split), tests-only files/ pin the public API through child-pwsh driver scripts. PowerShell-native pins: psd1 manifest passing Test-ModuleManifest, Export-ModuleMember discipline (Get-Command -Module OpsKit == exactly 4 names), ValueFromPipeline vs parameter binding pinned identical for two functions, SupportsShouldProcess with -WhatIf pinned byte-exact via directory snapshots, PSObject.Properties property-bag contract under StrictMode. Differentiation: ps-depotops (6P7-1) is a REFACTOR of a shipped monolith into a module with behavior frozen -- here nothing ships and the module design itself is the task, plus pipeline binding and ShouldProcess which depotops never touches; ps-inidrift/ps-svcplan are flat build scripts (INI drift text report / service action planner) with no module surface; wave-4 module-split analogs (py-clientsplit, ts-mailroster) have no manifest/export mechanics. Catalog wave-4 sibling: chunks 10/12 repo-scale capstones.
